title: Meningitis & Encephalitis
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Meningitis & Encephalitis
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Meningitis & Encephalitis
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Meningitis & Encephalitis** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Meningitis & Encephalitis. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Meningitis & Encephalitis, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Meningitis & Encephalitis.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Meningitis & Encephalitis
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: IDSA Practice Guidelines for Bacterial Meningitis

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Meningitis & Encephalitis?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Meningitis & Encephalitis, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Meningitis & Encephalitis?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Meningitis & Encephalitis, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Meningitis & Encephalitis requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Meningitis & Encephalitis requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CT before LP only if: altered mental status, immunocompromised, papilledema, focal neuro deficit, seizure
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: CT usually normal in uncomplicated meningitis; MRI shows leptomeningeal enhancement
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Don't delay antibiotics for imaging; CT clears for safe LP; MRI shows complications (abscess, ventriculitis)
